g.text: Adjustment for local fecal strength and population size
shedding kinetics (i.e., shedding onset time distribution and shedding load distribution) estimated with
to compute epidemic risk for Georgia, Michigan, and Texas, because of the absence
of clinical surveillance data in the corresponding catchment areas of wastewater surveillance.
Notably, the shedding load distribution estimated with California data contain factors related to two local
characteristics, namely fecal load and population size in the state.
Therefore, it is required to adjust these factors when applying it to other regions in estimating
and epidemic risk. To adjust these factors, we used the following equation
